og.title: Ultraviolet Light and Fluorescence | CARSI
og.description: A free introductory course on ultraviolet light and fluorescence for water loss inspections: UVA, UVB and UVC properties, UV safety, equipment selection, and moisture and microbial detection.

faq.q: How is UV light used in water loss inspections?
faq.a: Ultraviolet light can trigger fluorescence in moisture, dye tracers and residues, helping trace leak sources, map porous saturation, verify drying progress and flag possible microbial growth sites.

faq.q: What is the difference between UVA, UVB and UVC?
faq.a: UVA (315–400 nm) is lower energy and needs fewer precautions, UVB (280–315 nm) produces more visible fluorescence but requires more shielding, and UVC (below 280 nm) is germicidal and demands strict safety protocols.

faq.q: Is UV light safe to use during inspections?
faq.a: UV exposure carries skin and eye risks, so protective eyewear, appropriate clothing and routine exposure monitoring are used, with greater care needed for shorter UVB and UVC wavelengths.

faq.q: Can UV replace other moisture detection methods?
faq.a: No. UV works best alongside complementary tools such as infrared thermography and moisture mapping, which help confirm findings and locate hidden moisture not visible under UV alone.
